settings.gg_Ttq2kJj9_text:
How do I apply it? Once daily, at night, after washing your face. Apply to face AND neck—don't forget the neck. Takes approximately 30 seconds. No complicated routine required.


Will it feel greasy? No. The formulation absorbs completely within 60-90 seconds. You won't feel residue on your pillow. This was a specific design requirement—Italian men don't tolerate greasy skincare any more than American men do.


What if I have sensitive skin? Retinyl Palmitate was specifically chosen because it's gentler than standard Retinol. The formulation is dermatologically tested for sensitive skin. If you've had reactions to harsh Retinol products before, this is the alternative.


When will I see results? Initial improvements in texture and hydration: Week 1-2. Visible structural firming in the neck and jawline: Week 6-8. This timeline is based on collagen synthesis biology and cannot be accelerated.


Is there a subscription? No. This is a one-time purchase. No auto-ship, no recurring charges, no "cancel before you're charged" games. If you want more after your first two bottles, you order more. If you don't, you don't. Transactional integrity.


What if it doesn't work for me? Request a refund within 60 days. Full refund, no questions beyond basic verification. The guarantee exists specifically because the company is confident most men will see results.
settings.gg_gLeAjaKd_text: "I have weird sun spots on my face and my neck was sagging badly. The spots faded within 2 weeks, but the real change was by week 6—my neck looked tighter, more defined. My collar fits better. I stand differently now."
settings.ggfz4834Q_0_text:
I've spent 23 years watching men struggle with something that shouldn't be this complicated.


The science of skin aging is well understood. The mechanisms that reverse it are established. The formulations that work have existed for decades.


The problem has never been knowledge. It's been access—and the courage to try something different after being disappointed so many times.


If your neck bothers you every time you look in the mirror... if you've started avoiding certain angles in photographs... if you feel like your outside no longer matches your inside...


You don't have to accept that.


The Italian Protocol exists. It works for most men who commit to the full 8 weeks. And with the guarantee, there's no financial risk in finding out if you're one of them.


The only risk is spending another year watching the problem get worse.


I hope you'll make the decision that's right for you.


— Dr. Marco Bianchi Pharmacist, Rome 23 years of practice
settings.gggDVlmhqVN_text: 🇮🇹 Made in Italy | Approved by Italian Pharmacists | 60-Day Money-Back Guarantee

settings.ggOKnR2Om-W_text: The information provided on this page is for educational purposes and should not be construed as medical advice. Individual results may vary based on skin type, consistency of use, and biological factors. If you have specific dermatological conditions, consult with a healthcare provider before beginning any new skincare regimen. Dr. Marco Bianchi's recommendations are based on his professional experience and review of the Omnis Uomo formulation.

settings.gg-s1U-a7ON_text:
In Italy, the pharmacy is different from what you have in America.


We don't just fill prescriptions and sell candy bars. Italian pharmacists are trained as frontline healthcare advisors. People come to us first—before the doctor—for skin conditions, digestive issues, sleep problems... and yes, aging.


Over the past two decades, I've counseled thousands of men about their skin. And I've noticed something peculiar:


When American tourists come into my pharmacy asking for anti-aging advice, they always ask the same question:
settings.gg04un1EaV4_text: I'm writing this for a specific type of man. You'll know if this is for you:
settings.gg1mNq57ffX_text: The most powerful product in the world is useless if you quit after a week because your face is peeling off.
settings.gg2tIVaQMN6_text: 2. Something gentle enough to use consistently
settings.ggAPKnnAGTy_text:
In the past two years, I've seen a strange trend among American men: beef tallow.


They come into my pharmacy asking about it. "It's what our grandfathers used," they tell me. "It's all natural. No chemicals."


I try to be diplomatic. But inside, I'm shaking my head.


Here's the truth about beef tallow: It's passive.


Tallow is an occlusive. That means it sits on TOP of your skin and creates a barrier. It prevents moisture from escaping. That's all it does.


It doesn't penetrate. It doesn't signal your cells to do anything. It doesn't stimulate collagen production.


It's like putting a tarp over a crumbling building. The collapse continues underneath—you just can't see it as clearly.


And that's the best-case scenario.


The worst case? Tallow is highly comedogenic. It clogs pores. I've seen men come in with cystic acne along their jawline after using it for months. Men who hadn't had acne since they were teenagers.


Plus—and I say this delicately—it smells like a steakhouse. Your wife will not appreciate it. Neither will your pillowcase.


There's a reason Italian pharmacies don't stock beef tallow. It's not medicine. It's cooking fat.
settings.ggAqfECSQ3Y_text:
It ages faster than the rest of your face.


The skin on your neck is thinner. It has fewer oil glands. It gets less attention, less protection, less care.


And after 50? The collagen loss accelerates dramatically in this area. The underlying structure collapses. Gravity takes over.


You wake up one morning and suddenly there's this... looseness... that wasn't there before.


Most men don't even notice it happening. Until they see a photo from their daughter's wedding. Or catch their reflection in a Zoom call. Or a colleague makes a comment about them "looking tired."


Then they panic. They buy whatever cream promises "younger skin." They slather it on their face.


And they completely ignore the neck.


That's mistake #1. But it's not the only one.
settings.ggAvlCP674P_text: "What can I do about these wrinkles?"
settings.ggCNYyuVOIk_text: If that sounds familiar, keep reading. Because what I'm about to share took me over two decades to fully understand—and it explains why the American skincare industry has been failing men like you for years.
settings.ggCjS8yomP__text:
Let me be direct with you.


The American skincare industry doesn't understand men over 50. Or worse—they understand you perfectly, and they're exploiting your desperation.


They know you'll buy whatever promises "younger skin." They know you don't have time to research ingredients. They know you'll try something for a few weeks, see no results, and move on to the next product.


That cycle is their business model.


They don't need to fix your neck. They just need to sell you hope in a jar.


Here's what I've seen fail, over and over again:
settings.ggG2lm4gsWE_text:
"Stop buying products designed for women. Stop chasing trends. Stop believing that 'natural' automatically means 'effective.'"


"Your neck needs a specific type of intervention. One that Italian men have been using for years—quietly, without fanfare, while American companies were busy repackaging the same basic moisturizers."


"Would you like to know what we recommend?"


Most of them lean in at this point. Because they've tried everything else. And they're finally ready to listen.
settings.ggGRLaXWjf__text: Not surface-level moisture. Deep, cellular signaling that tells your fibroblasts to start building new collagen scaffolding again.
settings.ggLWhXFfn6S_text: Italian Pharmacist Reveals: The #1 Sign That Makes Men Look "Old" Isn't Wrinkles—It's Something Most Products Completely Ignore
settings.ggNeqzC4IAz_text:
This is the most common mistake I see.


A man notices his neck is sagging. He walks into CVS or Walgreens. He grabs a bottle of Nivea or CeraVe or whatever his wife uses. He applies it every night for a month.


Nothing changes.


Here's why: Moisturizers don't rebuild structure. They just add water.


Think of your skin like a building. Collagen is the steel framework—the scaffolding that holds everything up. When that framework weakens, the building sags.


A moisturizer is like giving that building a fresh coat of paint. It might look slightly better on the surface. But the structure is still collapsing underneath.


Your neck isn't dry. It's structurally compromised.


No amount of hydration will fix that.
settings.ggPEGV--92o_text: This is what I tell the American men who visit my pharmacy:
settings.ggQFv0ZZ8m__text: Most men don't know this: there are enzymes in your skin called MMPs that actively break down collagen. If you're building new collagen but not protecting it, you're running on a treadmill.
settings.ggSdZqFlz3Q_text: The #1 thing that makes a man look "old" isn't wrinkles. It's the neck.
settings.ggTPUqTHDTg_text: 3. Something that protects the collagen you're building
settings.ggWXljIZlpy_text: After 23 years, I've identified exactly three things a man's aging neck actually needs:
settings.ggdcISJ7MsW_text: My name is Dr. Marco Bianchi. I've been a pharmacist in Rome for 23 years.
settings.gggBa_bfd_f_text:
Some men skip the creams entirely and go straight to the dermatologist's office.


Botox. Fillers. Laser treatments. Neck lifts.


I understand the appeal. You want results now. You have the money. Why not just fix it directly?


Here's what they don't tell you:
settings.gggxdU3Lhmz_text:
I'm not saying these options are wrong for everyone. But they're not the first step. They're the last resort.


The first step is rebuilding the structural foundation. If you don't do that, nothing else matters.
settings.ggjpPQS5l0N_text:
Now, here's where things get complicated.


Retinol—Vitamin A—is actually one of the few ingredients proven to rebuild collagen. The science is solid. Dermatologists have been recommending it for decades.


So why do I call it a "mistake"?


Because most Retinol products are too harsh for men over 50.


Let me explain.


Pharmaceutical-grade Retinol works by forcing rapid cell turnover. It essentially tells your skin: "Shed faster.

 Rebuild faster."


In theory, this sounds great. In practice? For a man in his 50s or 60s with thinner, more sensitive skin?


It's a disaster.


I've seen it dozens of times. A man starts using a Retinol cream he bought online. Within a week, his face is red.

 Peeling. Burning. Flaking skin everywhere.


He looks like he stuck his face in a furnace.


And the neck area? Even worse. The skin there is more delicate. It reacts more severely.


So what does he do? He stops using the product. Goes back to his useless moisturizer. And tells himself that "anti-aging stuff doesn't work."


The problem wasn't the concept. It was the execution.


He needed the collagen-rebuilding benefits of Vitamin A. But he needed it in a form his skin could actually tolerate.
settings.ggmR7qlD9g7_text:
Find a formulation that does all three, and you'll see results. Real, visible, structural results.


Ignore any one of them, and you'll keep wasting money on products that don't work.
settings.ggp4c0exzuk_text: After 23 years dispensing skincare in Rome, I finally understand why American men spend billions on anti-aging products... and still look older every year.
settings.ggpUJXtvv0K_text: Here's what nobody tells you about the neck:
settings.ggq-t6w_CuD_text:
They point to their forehead. Their crow's feet. The lines around their mouth.


And every time, I have to stop myself from saying what I'm actually thinking:


"Signore... the wrinkles aren't your problem."
settings.ggwY3K1iKUH_text:
This may surprise you. It surprised me too, when I first started noticing the pattern.


But after 23 years of studying faces—thousands of them—I can tell you with absolute certainty:


A man can have wrinkles on his forehead and still look distinguished, vital, even handsome.


But a man with a sagging neck? A soft, undefined jawline? Loose skin hanging below the chin?


He looks old. Tired. Like he's given up.


It doesn't matter how expensive his suit is. Doesn't matter how fit his body is. Doesn't matter how sharp his mind is.


The neck tells a different story. And people believe what they see.


I call it "il collo del tacchino"—the turkey neck. And it's the silent killer of a man's presence.
settings.ggxR9A0IYC8_childItem_0: Botox doesn't work on sagging skin. Botox paralyzes muscles—it's designed for expression lines, not structural collapse. Your neck isn't sagging because of muscle movement. It's sagging because the collagen framework has degraded.
settings.ggxR9A0IYC8_childItem_1: Fillers are temporary and expensive. You're looking at $500-2,000 per treatment, every 6-12 months. And if it's done poorly? You get that puffy, "pillow face" look that screams "I've had work done."
settings.ggxR9A0IYC8_childItem_2: Surgical neck lifts work, but... You're talking $5,000-15,000. Weeks of recovery. Potential complications. And even then, without ongoing collagen support, the sagging will return within a few years.

Here's what happens when you apply it to your skin:
settings.ggJZWJHbFDq_text:
This is the philosophy behind Italian anti-aging formulation. It's not enough to stimulate. You must also protect.


American products typically focus on one or the other. They'll give you a Retinol product (stimulation) and tell you to use a separate moisturizer (protection). Two products. Two price tags. Two opportunities for something to go wrong.


Italian pharmaceutical tradition combines them into a single, synergistic formula. One application. One ritual. Complete intervention.


And we apply it at night—when your skin's natural repair processes are most active. The circadian rhythm of cellular regeneration peaks between 11pm and 2am. That's when your skin is primed to receive these signals.


This isn't complicated. It's just correct.

Within 18 months, Omnis had become the #1 pharmacist recommendation for men over 40 in Italy. Not through television advertising or celebrity endorsements—through word of mouth among pharmacists and their patients.


Today, the numbers are difficult to ignore:
settings.ggW24EKL39R_text: The skin on your neck has unique characteristics that make it particularly suited to this three-part approach:
settings.ggX9kCC9axZ_text: Function: Strengthens and stabilizes new collagen fibers
settings.ggXhf_YZaYi_text:
In the 1980s, Italian dermatological researchers were studying Vitamin A derivatives. They knew that Retinoic Acid—the active form of Vitamin A—was the gold standard for collagen rebuilding. The science was clear.


But they also knew the problem: Retinoic Acid is too aggressive for most skin.


It works by forcing immediate, rapid cell turnover. For young skin, this is tolerable. For mature skin—especially the thin, sensitive skin of the neck—it's often too much. Redness. Peeling. Inflammation. Damaged moisture barriers.


So they asked: "What if we could deliver the same collagen-building benefit, but through a gentler pathway?"


The answer was Retinyl Palmitate.
settings.ggZ9E6xBzUL_text: These aren't marketing statistics manufactured by an advertising agency. These are outcomes tracked by pharmacists across Italy who recommend this product to their patients and follow up on results.
settings.ggdmJEfQCGx_text: Your fibroblasts begin producing new Type I collagen—the same structural protein that's been degrading since you turned 50. Over 6-8 weeks, this new collagen accumulates, creating visible firming and tightening.
settings.ggj7C6LOfOQ_text: "What does aging male skin actually need at the cellular level—and how do we deliver it without the side effects?"
settings.gglxA_VI2Ls_text:
This is where most American products fail. They include one active ingredient at a low concentration and call it a day.


Italian pharmaceutical formulation is different. We think in systems.


If you're going to rebuild collagen, you need three things working together:
settings.ggsOMXh8XZ2_text:
Here's something most men don't know: newly produced collagen is fragile. It needs to be "cross-linked" and stabilized to become strong, durable structural protein.


Vitamin C is the essential cofactor for this process. It's like the difference between wet concrete and cured concrete. Without adequate Vitamin C, your new collagen won't set properly.


But here's the problem: Vitamin C is notoriously unstable. It oxidizes quickly when exposed to air and light. Most Vitamin C products have degraded significantly before they even reach your skin.


Italian labs solved this decades ago using stabilized forms—like Sodium Ascorbyl Phosphate—that remain potent until the moment of application. The best formulations use Vitamin C derived from Sicilian blood oranges, which has a unique antioxidant profile.
settings.ggtQn0W-A3k_text: The Retinoic Acid binds to receptors on your fibroblast cells. These are the "construction workers" of your skin. The signal tells them: "Start building new collagen."
settings.ggtt2biO-LY_text: What happened next surprised even me.
settings.ggv66qJ0Hsx_text: Function: Blocks collagen-destroying enzymes while providing deep hydration
settings.ggvP-hfgjQg_text: How Retinyl Palmitate works (for the skeptics who want to understand)
settings.ggwni7HxvFi_text:
This is where most people misunderstand Hyaluronic Acid.


Yes, it hydrates. Yes, it "plumps" fine lines temporarily. But that's not why we include it.


The real function of Hyaluronic Acid in an anti-aging formulation is enzyme inhibition.


Remember those MMPs I mentioned—the enzymes that break down collagen? Hyaluronic Acid has been shown to inhibit MMP activity. It's actively defending the collagen you're building from being broken down.


Think of it as posting a security guard at the construction site. You're building new structure with Retinyl Palmitate, strengthening it with Vitamin C, and protecting it from demolition with Hyaluronic Acid.
settings.ggz_DcY9K-l_text: Unlike harsh Retinol that attacks the surface immediately, Retinyl Palmitate absorbs slowly and deeply into the dermis—the structural layer of your skin where collagen actually lives.

settings.gg02mqLTMVi_text:
Continued use maintains and builds upon these results. The protective elements prevent degradation while the Retinyl Palmitate continues supporting collagen synthesis.

settings.gg2L5Ik6AEj_text:
I always tell my patients: the 8-week timeline is not arbitrary.


Collagen synthesis is a biological process. It cannot be rushed. Anyone who promises you visible structural change in "3 days" or "one week" is lying to you. They're selling you temporary surface effects—hydration, mild swelling from irritation—not actual rebuilding.


Real structural change requires real biological time. The men who see the best results are those who trust the process and apply consistently for the full 8 weeks.


This is why I appreciate that Omnis offers their supply in a two-month package. It ensures you have enough product to complete the full transformation cycle without interruption.

settings.ggAGD0WmdLZ_text:
Because Italian pharmaceutical regulations are stricter than American cosmetic regulations.


The FDA classifies most skincare as "cosmetics," which means minimal oversight. Companies can make broad claims with little substantiation. They can include ingredients that are banned in Europe.


Italian pharmaceutical standards require efficacy testing, stability testing, and ingredient purity verification. The formulation process is closer to medicine than makeup.


This isn't nationalism. It's regulatory reality. When I recommend a product to a patient, I need to know it meets pharmaceutical standards—not just marketing standards.
settings.ggAdEtW4dAd_text:

settings.ggCFkMVyKRV_text:
You don't. Not with certainty. And anyone who guarantees specific results for your specific biology is lying to you.


What I can tell you is this:


The mechanism is sound. Retinyl Palmitate stimulates collagen production. Vitamin C stabilizes that collagen. Hyaluronic Acid protects it from enzymatic breakdown. This is established dermatological science, not speculation.


72,000+ men have used this formulation. 87% report visible improvement in the neck and jawline area. The probability that you'll see meaningful results is high.


But biology is individual. Which is why Omnis offers a 60-day guarantee. You have enough time to complete the full 8-week transformation cycle. If you don't see results, you get your money back.


That's as close to "risk-free" as medicine gets.

settings.ggDZYp1BRpq_text:
Then you've experienced exactly why Retinyl Palmitate exists.


Standard Retinol—and especially prescription Tretinoin—works by forcing rapid, aggressive cell turnover. For mature skin, this is often too much. The irritation causes most men to quit before seeing results.


Retinyl Palmitate converts to the active form inside your skin, gradually, through a two-step enzymatic process. You get the same collagen-building outcome without the surface-level assault.


If harsh Retinol is a sledgehammer, Retinyl Palmitate is a precision instrument. Same destination, gentler path.
settings.ggG4Fx95u4C_text: If you're ready to begin the Italian Protocol:
settings.ggGGObA0NQR_text:
Commit to 60 days. Apply Omnis every night before bed. Give the formulation time to work—the full 8 weeks required for collagen rebuilding.


If it works (as it has for 72,000+ men), you'll see visible tightening in your neck. A more defined jawline. The "turkey neck" appearance reduced. You'll look in the mirror and see something closer to the man you still feel like inside.


If it doesn't work, you request a refund and you're back where you started—minus nothing except two months of trying.


Your risk: Zero dollars (with the guarantee)

 

Your potential outcome: A neck that doesn't make you self-conscious anymore

settings.ggIExn0fks9_childItem_0: "Why should I trust an Italian product over American brands?"
settings.ggJjx-cHJf2_text: — A $40 value. 120 pages on style, presence, and the Italian approach to aging well. (Not required reading, but my patients tell me they appreciate it.)
settings.ggMtiDa3D9f_text:
As a pharmacist, I never recommend a product without understanding the guarantee terms. Here's what Omnis offers:


Try it for 60 days. That's the full 8-week transformation timeline—enough time for the collagen rebuilding process to produce visible structural change.


If you don't see results, request a full refund. Contact the support team, explain that you completed the protocol and didn't see the improvement you expected, and receive every penny back.


No "restocking fees." No complicated return authorization process. No interrogation about why you're returning it.


This is how confident they are in the formulation.


In 23 years of pharmacy practice, I've learned that guarantee terms tell you everything about a company's confidence in their product. Companies that offer short windows (14 days, 30 days) know their products don't produce lasting results. They're hoping you'll forget to return it.


A 60-day guarantee on an 8-week protocol means they expect you to complete the full cycle and see results. They're betting on efficacy, not forgetfulness.


Your financial risk: Zero.
settings.ggRnxZc8qvR_text: Let me be direct with you—as I would be with any patient in my pharmacy.

settings.ggT0Npug-g4_text: The Complete Three-Part System in One Application:
settings.ggTLaMg6PdB_text:
Close this page. Go back to your current routine—whether that's nothing, or a moisturizer that isn't working, or occasional consideration of expensive procedures you're not ready to commit to.


Your neck will continue on its current trajectory. Collagen production will continue declining at approximately 1-2% per year. The sagging will progress. The "turkey neck" will become more pronounced.


This isn't a scare tactic. It's biology. After 50, this process doesn't reverse itself without intervention.


Your risk: Zero dollars (today)

 

Your guaranteed outcome: Continued structural decline
settings.ggXpxF9En3n_text:
No. And the fact that you're asking tells me you've been burned before.


Here's how to tell the difference: Look at the mechanism.


A moisturizer adds water to the surface. That's all it does. The effects disappear when the water evaporates.


Omnis contains Retinyl Palmitate—a Vitamin A derivative that signals your cells to produce new collagen. This is structural change, not surface hydration. The effects accumulate over time because you're literally rebuilding the scaffolding of your skin.


If a product doesn't contain an active that stimulates collagen synthesis, it's a moisturizer. No matter what the label says.
settings.ggcXrrxy20x_text:
I don't tell my patients what to do. I give them information and let them decide.


But when they ask me directly—"Marco, what would you do?"—I tell them the truth:


I would try it.


Not because it's guaranteed to work for everyone. Nothing in medicine is guaranteed.


But because the mechanism is sound, the risk is zero with the guarantee, and the cost of inaction is watching yourself age in a way that bothers you.


I've seen too many men spend years "thinking about it" while their necks continued to sag. They eventually try something—often something more expensive and more invasive—and wish they'd started sooner.


The best time to address structural aging was 10 years ago. The second best time is now.


If you're still reading this, you already know something needs to change. The only question is whether you're ready to find out if this is the solution.
settings.ggeW77cnUjg_childItem_0: "I've tried Retinol before and it destroyed my skin."
settings.ggfInSJYpim_childItem_0: Less than $1.10 per day — Less than your morning coffee
settings.gghcLNjCJ6i_text: That's six full months of supply—enough to complete the entire transformation timeline.
settings.ggih4N2SYCJ_text:
Compared to what?


A drugstore moisturizer that doesn't work? Yes, it's more expensive.


A neck lift consultation? That's $200 just for the appointment. The procedure itself is $5,000-15,000.


Botox for the neck area? $300-600 per session, every 3-4 months. That's $1,200-2,400 per year, indefinitely.


The "prescription-strength" Retinol your dermatologist might recommend? $150-300 per tube, plus the office visit.


At $70 for a two-month supply, Omnis costs approximately $1.10 per day. That's less than your morning coffee.


The question isn't whether $70 is expensive. The question is: expensive compared to the alternative? Compared to doing nothing and watching your neck continue to sag?


I've never had a patient tell me they regretted spending $70 on something that worked.
settings.ggmTsojTDn-_text: So here is the decision, as I present it to my patients:
settings.ggoS3HsedfD_childItem_0: Retinyl Palmitate (Vitamin A) — The "gentleman's Retinol." Signals fibroblast cells to restart collagen production without the burning, peeling, or irritation of harsh retinoids.
settings.ggoS3HsedfD_childItem_1: Stabilized Vitamin C from Sicilian Oranges — Cross-links and strengthens new collagen fibers. Remains potent until moment of application (unlike unstable Vitamin C serums).
settings.ggoS3HsedfD_childItem_2: Hyaluronic Acid — Inhibits MMP enzymes that break down collagen. Provides deep hydration while protecting your structural investment.
settings.ggojKT5i0GF_text:
I've spent 23 years helping men navigate the confusing landscape of skincare. I've seen thousands of products come and go. I've watched men waste money on solutions that never had a chance of working.


And I've learned that the men who get results are the ones who stop hoping and start deciding.


So let me give you the same assessment I give to the men who visit my pharmacy in Rome:
settings.ggpqo8bkoQ8_childItem_0: "What if it doesn't work? What if I'm wasting my money again?"
settings.ggu3gqC17Qk_text:
Then you return it and get every penny back.


Omnis offers a 60-day money-back guarantee. Not 30 days—60. That's specifically because the transformation timeline requires 8 weeks. They're giving you enough time to complete the full cycle before you decide.


If you don't see visible improvement in your neck by week 8, you contact their support team and receive a full refund. No complicated process. No "restocking fees." No interrogation.


You're not risking $70. You're risking nothing except the time it takes to find out if this works for you.


And if it does work? You've solved a problem that's been bothering you every time you look in the mirror.
